concepts.title: SQL Syntax
concepts.description: SQL syntax is case-insensitive, but it is recommended to use standard capitalization for better readability and maintainability.

concepts.title: SQL Commands
concepts.description: SQL commands are used to perform various operations on a database, including creating, modifying, and querying data. Common SQL commands include SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, and DROP.

concepts.title: SQL WHERE Clause
concepts.description: The WHERE clause is used to filter data based on conditions. It is used with SELECT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements to apply conditions to the data being retrieved or modified.

concepts.title: SQL Query Structure
concepts.description: SQL query structure can be remembered using the mnemonic 'SELECT FROM WHERE': SELECT - what to retrieve FROM - where to retrieve FROM WHERE - conditions to apply

concepts.title: SQL Injection
concepts.description: SQL injection occurs when user input is not properly sanitized and is used directly in a SQL query, allowing an attacker to execute malicious SQL code. To prevent SQL injection, use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

concepts.title: SQL Data Types
concepts.description: Common SQL data types include INTEGER, VARCHAR, CHAR, DATE, TIME, and TIMESTAMP. Understanding the differences between these data types is crucial for designing and implementing efficient database systems.

concepts.title: SQL Indexing
concepts.description: SQL indexing is used to improve the performance of queries by allowing the database to quickly locate specific data. Common indexing techniques include B-tree indexing and hash indexing.

concepts.title: SQL Constraints
concepts.description: SQL constraints are used to enforce data integrity by restricting the values that can be inserted or modified in a database. Common constraints include PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, and CHECK constraints.

concepts.title: SQL JOINs
concepts.description: SQL JOINs are used to combine data from multiple tables based on a common column. Common JOIN types include INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, and RIGHT JOIN.
